narrative_record_distortion.label: Narrative / Record Distortion
narrative_record_distortion.purpose: How the record tells, recycles, distorts, or hardens a story over time or across documents.

macros.label: Recycled Allegation Pattern
macros.definition: An allegation, fact, or characterization from an earlier record reappears in a later document as if newly verified, without independent re-assessment.
lookFor: Events described with temporal language (recently, again) that actually occurred years ago
Prior hospitalizations or crises cited as current risk evidence without documented recurrence
Diagnostic conclusions or behavioral descriptions carried forward without re-evaluation
A single incident from the distant past used as the primary basis for current authorization
macros.avoid: Do not tag merely because a prior event is mentioned. Only tag when the prior event is presented as current evidence without independent re-verification.

macros.label: Narrative Drift
macros.definition: The description of an event, condition, or behavior changes meaningfully between documents or across time, without documented explanation for the change.
lookFor: A prognosis that shifts from favorable to guarded with no new clinical assessment
A behavior characterized positively in one document and negatively in another without explanation
A diagnosis that changes between documents without documented clinical reasoning
A period described as stable in one document and as non-compliant in a subsequent one
macros.avoid: Do not tag when a documented change in condition or circumstances explicitly explains the shift in characterization.

macros.label: Source Confusion
macros.definition: The source of a claim is unclear, hearsay-based, attributed to an unidentified party, undated, or misattributed to the wrong document or author.
lookFor: The family reports, third parties describe, nursing notes indicate — without attachment of signed or sworn statements
Undated documents or notes cited as evidence
References to exhibits or reports not appended to the file
Claims attributed to unnamed or unidentified staff members
macros.avoid: Do not tag standard clinical notes clearly attributed to identifiable, named authors within the record.

macros.label: Biographical Rewriting
macros.definition: The person's personal, social, or medical history is reformulated in ways that diverge materially from earlier documented facts, without explanation.
lookFor: Positive facts from earlier records (employment, social contacts, therapeutic engagement) absent or reframed negatively in later documents
A stable period characterized as non-compliance without supporting documentation
A prior voluntary engagement reframed as crisis-driven or coerced
Social network described as supportive in an earlier document and as destabilizing in a later one
macros.avoid: Do not tag when new documented events explicitly explain why the earlier characterization no longer applies.

macros.label: Claim Canonization
macros.definition: A contested, unverified, or one-sided claim is repeated across the record until it appears established as objective fact.
lookFor: A characterization that first appeared as an allegation and now appears as a documented fact without intermediate verification
Repeated use of the same evaluative language across multiple documents without new sourcing
Behavioral labels applied in one document and copied into subsequent documents as established
The person's own counter-narrative consistently omitted while the institutional account is reproduced
macros.avoid: Do not tag facts that have been independently verified through multiple sources or acknowledged by the person themselves.

macros.label: Record Contradiction
macros.definition: A statement in the document directly contradicts another statement in the same document or in a companion document, without acknowledgment or resolution.
lookFor: A document that acknowledges a stabilizing fact in one section and ignores it in the clinical conclusion
Two documents that describe the same period or event in incompatible terms
A denial of risk or symptom in one document and assertion of imminent risk in another, covering the same period
The person described as calm and cooperative in one note and as agitated and dangerous in an adjacent note for the same encounter
macros.avoid: Do not tag when the record acknowledges and explicitly explains the apparent contradiction.

clinical_diagnostic_failure.label: Clinical / Diagnostic Failure
clinical_diagnostic_failure.purpose: How clinical necessity, risk, diagnosis, causality, trauma, coercion, and validation are constructed or avoided in the record.

macros.label: Refusal of Objective Validation
macros.definition: A clinical conclusion — about diagnosis, capacity, risk, or prognosis — is asserted without the objective evaluation, testing, or assessment that would normally be required to support it.
lookFor: A capacity conclusion without neuropsychological assessment or standardized capacity screening
A risk conclusion without recent incident documentation or validated risk instrument
A prognosis stated with certainty without reference to outcome data or comparative cases
A diagnosis presented as established without documented structured assessment
macros.avoid: Do not tag every absence of testing. Focus on situations where objective validation is materially required by clinical standards and is explicitly missing.

macros.label: Failure of Differential Diagnosis
macros.definition: A diagnosis or clinical conclusion is presented without documentation of alternative explanations that were considered and ruled out.
lookFor: A single diagnosis asserted without discussion of what else was considered
A diagnostic change from one document to another with no clinical explanation
Behavioral presentations attributed to psychiatric cause without consideration of situational, relational, or organic factors
A conclusion of incapacity without examination of partial capacity or supported decision-making alternatives
macros.avoid: Do not tag when the record explicitly considers and rules out alternative diagnoses or explanations, even briefly.

macros.label: Etiological Closure
macros.definition: A single cause is asserted for the person's current condition or behavior without considering alternative explanations, contextual factors, or contributing history.
lookFor: All current behavior attributed entirely to the psychiatric diagnosis without considering situational or relational context
A crisis attributed solely to non-compliance without examining what may have precipitated the episode
Historical patterns explained exclusively through the psychiatric frame without examining other plausible causes
Refusal of treatment explained only as symptom without considering prior adverse treatment experiences
macros.avoid: Do not tag when the record genuinely addresses multiple contributing factors, even if imperfectly.

macros.label: Iatrogenic Cause Minimization
macros.definition: The potential role of prior treatment, medication, institutionalization, or care system contact in producing or worsening the person's current presentation is not considered.
lookFor: Side effect complaints or concerns not documented or addressed in the clinical record
Deterioration following a medication change not analyzed for causal connection
A person's treatment refusal explained only as symptom without considering documented prior adverse effects
Institutional contact cited as a stabilizing factor without examination of whether it may have contributed to the current episode
macros.avoid: Do not tag when the record explicitly examines and rules out iatrogenic contributions to the current presentation.

macros.label: Risk Inflation Under Uncertainty
macros.definition: Risk or dangerousness is asserted with greater certainty or severity than the documented evidence supports, often by projecting from historical events to current risk.
lookFor: Imminent danger asserted without a recent specific incident documented in the current file
Historical crises described as evidence of current risk without documented recurrence
Probability language (will deteriorate, cannot safely return) used without structured risk assessment or actuarial basis
A request for an extended authorization period without clinical justification for the duration
macros.avoid: Do not tag when the risk conclusion is explicitly grounded in recent documented incidents, validated risk instruments, or clear clinical reasoning.

macros.label: Trauma Deferral
macros.definition: Traumatic history, adverse experiences, or potentially explanatory life events are present in the record but are not incorporated into the clinical reasoning or differential.
lookFor: Notes about adverse history or traumatic events that do not appear in the clinical formulation
Trauma-related behavior interpreted purely as psychiatric symptom without considering trauma etiology
Absence of any trauma-informed framing when trauma is documented in the same file
A person's distrust of institutions attributed to pathology rather than examined in light of documented institutional harm
macros.avoid: Do not tag when trauma is documented and the record explicitly incorporates it into the clinical reasoning, even partially.

macros.label: Coercion Through Ambiguity
macros.definition: Ambiguous behaviors, statements, or situations are consistently interpreted in ways that support compulsory measures, without considering alternative interpretations that would not support compulsion.
lookFor: Refusal of treatment characterized as a clinical symptom rather than a right or a reasoned position
Legal or procedural requests by the person characterized as evidence of pathology
Calm and cooperative behavior at intake acknowledged but then dismissed as not excluding future risk
The person's own explanation for their behavior systematically reinterpreted through a clinical frame that supports compulsion
macros.avoid: Do not tag when the ambiguous behavior is genuinely and immediately dangerous, or when the record explicitly examines and addresses non-compulsory alternatives.

macros.label: Root-Cause Avoidance
macros.definition: The underlying causes, contextual factors, or precipitating circumstances of the person's situation are not examined; only surface behaviors or presenting symptoms are documented and acted upon.
lookFor: No inquiry into housing, social, financial, relational, or systemic factors that may explain the current episode
No discussion of what specifically triggered the current hospitalization or application
Stabilizing factors from the person's life (employment, support networks, community engagement) not considered in the clinical assessment
A longitudinal record of interventions without any analysis of why stabilization has not been durable
macros.avoid: Do not tag when the record genuinely investigates contributing factors even if the inquiry is incomplete or the conclusions are disputed.
